member.name: Esther McVey
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Esther McVey is the Conservative MP for Tatton , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Folic Acid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the Government response of 29 June 2026 to the petition entitled End the mandatory fortification of white flour with folic acid immediately, what assessment he has made of the ade
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government introduced mandatory fortification of non-wholemeal wheat flour with folic acid as a preventative public health measure to help reduce the risk of neural tube defects (NTDs) during pregnancy. Increasing folic acid intake through fortificati...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Folic Acid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, pursuant to the Answer of 23 February 2026 to Question 113069 on Folic Acid, to clarify where the impact assessment has been published as it is not available on the cited link.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government introduced mandatory fortification of non-wholemeal wheat flour with folic acid as a preventative public health measure to help reduce the risk of neural tube defects (NTDs) during pregnancy. Increasing folic acid intake through fortificati...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government introduced mandatory fortification of non-wholemeal wheat flour with folic acid as a preventative public health measure to help reduce the risk of neural tube defects (NTDs) during pregnancy. Increasing folic acid intake through fortificati...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Revenue and Customs: Debts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how much money that is owed to HMRC by a) businesses and b) taxpayers has not been collected as a percentage over the previous five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) publishes information on the total amount of tax debt owed to it, but does not publish a breakdown of the debt balance between businesses and individual taxpayers. The Government is taking significant action to reduce tax deb...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Revenue and Customs: Debts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how much money is owed by a) businesses and b) taxpayers to HMRC i) this financial year, and ii) in the previous financial year, and when that money is expected to be paid back.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) publishes information on the total amount of tax debt owed to it, but does not publish a breakdown of the debt balance between businesses and individual taxpayers. The Government is taking significant action to reduce tax deb...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: John Edwards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether any recommendations to the ICO were made relating to workplace culture, leadership, governance or staff welfare in the findings of the independent workplace investigation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The independent investigation, and subsequent report, focused solely on the allegations made against Mr. Edwards. We are working closely with the ICO to consider what the next steps should be.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: John Edwards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what was the earliest date on which the Information Commissioner's Office informed her Department, or its predecessor sponsor Department, whether formally or informally, of any complain
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers were informed of workplace allegations relating to the Information Commissioner’s conduct on 16 February 2026.Ministers were not informed of any complaints prior to this.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: John Edwards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether the Executive Director for Communications and Public Affairs at the Information Commissioner's Office was interviewed by the independent workplace investigation of the former In
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is not appropriate to comment on who was interviewed as part of the investigation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Information Commissioner's Office: Public Appointments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of the appointments process undertaken for the new Information Commission Board.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are confident in the adequacy of the process that was followed to appoint non-executive members to the new Information Commission. The recruitment campaign was launched last June and has concluded. It was carried out in accordance with the public appoi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: John Edwards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether she intends to publish any further findings, recommendations and conclusions of the ICO independent workplace investigation, subject to any lawful redactions necessary to protec
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the right hon. Member to the answer I gave on 30 June 2026 to Question UIN 11634.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Information Commissioner's Office: Public Appointments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether recruitment for the next Information Commissioner has commenced; and if she will publish the expected timetable for an appointment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DSIT and the ICO have continued to work together on the transition to the Information Commission whilst the independent investigation was taking place. Due to the changes to the regulator’s governance structure, DSIT will be recruiting a Chair of the Info...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: John Edwards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether the independent workplace investigation of the former Information Commissioner, John Edwards, considered the actions and inaction of other senior Information Commissioner's Offi
writtenQuestions.answerText: The investigation focused solely on the allegations made against Mr Edwards.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Information Commissioner's Office: Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, with reference to the independent workplace assessment, whether she plans to require the Information Commissioner's Office to implement any recommendations relating to workplace culture
writtenQuestions.answerText: The allegations and subsequent independent investigation pertained to Mr Edwards alone; however, we are currently considering any further next steps.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: John Edwards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether she plans to publish the findings, recommendations and conclusions of the independent workplace investigation into John Edwards.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are looking at options following receipt of the report, working closely with the ICO who commissioned the investigation. It is important that any decision protects the witnesses and those who made the allegations.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Bank Services: Business
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will ensure the FCA reports on the number of businesses which close down each year due to a lack of banking facility, broken down by sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Generally speaking, it is economically beneficial for banks to provide business bank accounts, as this provides an important source of funding for their activities, such as funding their costs in relation to providing mortgages, consumer and business cred...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Department of Health and Social Care: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when he plans to answer Question 3280, tabled on 20 May 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the rt. Hon. Member to the answer I gave on 1 July 2026 to Question 3280.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, how many times her Department has responded to a written parliamentary question saying that the information is not held in 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested can be found at https://questions-statements.parliament.uk.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency has conducted searches and does not believe we hold a copy of the report referenced in FOI 903 from the UK Health Security Agency.The nature of the complaint in the report is outside the scope of wha...
